qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: HTTP server authentication bypass permits untrusted content injection into knowledge graph
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Identifies the same vulnerable code (src/server/http.ts:66-73 checkAuth function), same root cause (returns true when ENGRAM_API_TOKEN not set), same attack path (unauthenticated POST to /learn endpoint), and same impact (content injection into knowledge graph). The finding correctly describes the authentication bypass that enables CSRF-based graph exfiltration and persistent prompt injection as described in the advisory.

selected_finding.title: HTTP server authentication bypass permits untrusted content injection into knowledge graph

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding correctly identifies the advisory-described vulnerability: the HTTP server's checkAuth function at src/server/http.ts:66-73 returns true when ENGRAM_API_TOKEN is not set, completely bypassing authentication. The executed PoC demonstrates exploit success by successfully performing unauthenticated content injection via POST /learn, returning HTTP 201 Created. This matches the advisory's described attack scenario: browser-origin attacker can exfiltrate the knowledge graph and inject persistent prompt-injection payloads. The runtime artifacts (run.log and poc_execution.json) confirm the vulnerability was exploited as described.
evidence: Finding identifies the same vulnerable code location: src/server/http.ts:66-73 where checkAuth returns true when ENGRAM_API_TOKEN not set
Finding describes the same root cause: authentication completely bypassed when token not configured
Finding identifies the same attack path: unauthenticated HTTP POST to /learn endpoint
Finding describes the same impact: untrusted content injection into knowledge graph that surfaces to AI assistants
PoC execution succeeded: unauthenticated POST to /learn returned HTTP 201 Created
PoC logs confirm malicious payload was processed: [INJECTED] message logged by mock server
Runtime evidence satisfies exploit_success_predicate: unauthenticated content injection succeeded, matching the advisory's described CSRF-based attack

poc_executions.observed_effect: Mock HTTP server accepted unauthenticated POST to /learn endpoint and returned HTTP 201 Created. Server logs confirm the malicious content payload was received and processed. This demonstrates the advisory vulnerability: checkAuth() returns true when ENGRAM_API_TOKEN is not set, allowing complete authentication bypass.
